homeFaq.title: Straight answers. No sales, no guesswork.
homeFaq.description: We’re often brought in when things don’t add up. Here are the most common questions we’re asked.

items.question: Do you charge for an initial enquiry?
items.answer:
We don’t charge to talk things through initially.

If a survey is required, we’ll explain the scope so you understand what’s involved before anything is arranged.

All work is clearly defined in advance, so you know exactly what you’re getting.

items.question: Are you independent?
items.answer:
Yes — and that’s a key part of what we do.

Our surveys are diagnosis-led, focusing on identifying the root cause of the issue rather than selling a pre-selected treatment.

items.question: What does a damp survey include?
items.answer:
A typical survey includes:

– Inspection of affected and adjoining areas
– Moisture profiling to walls, floors, and structural elements
– Thermal imaging to identify cold surfaces and condensation risk
– Assessment of ventilation and internal environmental conditions
– External observations

You’ll receive a clear written report explaining what is happening, why it is happening, and what needs to be done.

items.question: Can you specify remedial works?
items.answer:
Yes.

We can produce a clear remedial specification, review contractor proposals, and help you understand whether suggested works align with the actual diagnosis.

items.question: What areas do you cover?
items.answer:
We cover London, Hertfordshire, Buckinghamshire, Bedfordshire Midlands and Oxfordshire.

We also take on projects further afield where the brief requires a specialist, independent assessment.

items.question: Can you help with mould and condensation issues?
items.answer:
Yes — but we approach these issues properly.

We assess whether mould is being driven by condensation, ventilation issues, thermal bridging, hidden leaks or wider building defects.

items.question: Do you work with homeowners only?
items.answer:
No.

We also support landlords, buyers, surveyors, managing agents and contractors who require an independent view.

items.question: Do I need a survey or just a quote?
items.answer:
In most cases, a survey is the correct starting point.

Damp and moisture issues are often misdiagnosed, and going straight to works without understanding the cause can lead to unnecessary or ineffective repairs.

serviceCards.title: Moisture, Damp & Mould Assessment
serviceCards.description:
Used where damp, mould or condensation is present and the cause is unclear.

We assess moisture behaviour, identify the source, and explain what is actually happening — not just the symptoms.
serviceCards.note: Where relevant, assessment considers principles aligned with BS 6576 for ground-related moisture behaviour.

serviceCards.title: Condensation & Ventilation Assessment
serviceCards.description:
Used where mould or condensation is recurring or linked to internal conditions.

We assess humidity, airflow, and cold surface risk to identify the root cause.
includes: Humidity behaviour and dew point risk
Thermal bridging and cold surface identification
serviceCards.note: Assessments are informed by BS 5250 principles for condensation and moisture control.

serviceCards.title: Leak Investigation & Water Ingress
serviceCards.description:
Used where water ingress is suspected from plumbing, roofing or external defects.

We identify the source and clearly distinguish between cause and resulting damage.
includes: Leak tracing and moisture spread assessment
Cause vs damage differentiation (critical for insurance claims)
CCTV drainage surveys where required to confirm defects and water pathways
serviceCards.note: Reporting is structured to support insurance claims, clearly defining cause, extent and required action.

serviceCards.title: External & Waterproofing Assessment
serviceCards.description:
Used where water ingress is linked to external construction, ground levels or waterproofing systems.

Includes terraces, façades, balconies and below-ground structures.
serviceCards.note: Assessments are aligned with BS 8102 guidance for below-ground and structural waterproofing.

serviceCards.title: Heritage Building Assessment & Planning Support
serviceCards.description:
Used for older or traditionally constructed buildings where modern damp treatments are often inappropriate.

We assess moisture behaviour and provide guidance suitable for conservation and planning.

serviceCards.title: Complex Investigations & Intrusive Assessment
serviceCards.description:
Used where the cause of moisture cannot be confirmed through non-invasive inspection alone.

We carry out targeted investigation to verify construction and identify hidden defects.
includes: CCTV and other investigative methods where necessary

serviceCards.title: Complex Cases & Second Opinions
serviceCards.description:
Used where previous reports or treatments have not resolved the issue.

We provide an independent review to identify misdiagnosis and establish the correct approach.
includes: Review of prior surveys and works
serviceCards.note: Often instructed where insurance claims have been disputed or where previous reports have lacked clarity.

serviceCards.title: Pre-Purchase Risk Assessments
serviceCards.description:
Used by buyers who need clarity before committing.

We identify risks, likely costs, and whether issues are manageable.

serviceCards.title: Commercial & Compliance Assessments
serviceCards.description:
Used for landlords, agents and commercial buildings requiring structured reporting.

Aligned with compliance requirements and risk management.
serviceCards.note: Reporting suitable for housing providers, insurers and regulatory requirements.

positioningBlock.title: Need a clear answer, not a guess or a generic quote?
positioningBlock.description: We provide proper diagnosis so you can understand what’s happening and what should be done — rather than jumping straight into unnecessary works.

remedialWorks.title: Remedial Works — Where Required
remedialWorks.description:
Our primary role is to diagnose the cause of moisture issues and define the correct solution.

Where remedial works are required, we can support or undertake their implementation — ensuring everything is aligned with the original diagnosis.

This includes both internal and external works, delivered in a controlled and proportionate way.
includes: External repairs and water ingress works
remedialWorks.closing: All works are based on evidence-led findings — not pre-defined systems or assumptions.

builtForClarity.title: Built for Clarity
builtForClarity.description:
Every client needs clarity, not guesswork.

We don’t offer quick fixes or push treatments. Our role is to understand what’s happening, explain it clearly, and help you make the right decision.

Each assessment is based on independent methodology, measurable data, and professional judgement — giving you conclusions you can rely on.

technicalStandards.title: Standards-informed approach
technicalStandards.description:
Our assessments are informed by recognised industry standards including BS 5250 (control of condensation), BS 8102 (below-ground waterproofing), and BS 6576 (rising damp), alongside practical building pathology.

This ensures our recommendations are technically grounded, proportionate, and appropriate to the building.

reporting.title: Reporting That Stands Up
reporting.description:
Our reports are structured to clearly define cause, extent and required action — not just describe visible symptoms.

This makes them suitable for use in insurance claims, disputes, and decision-making by landlords, agents and project teams.

hero.title: Common questions about damp investigations
hero.description: Helpful answers for homeowners, buyers, landlords and property teams who want to understand how independent damp diagnosis works.
hero.searchPlaceholder: Search questions about damp, mould or surveys...

faqs.question: What makes an independent damp survey different?
faqs.answer: The difference is independence from treatment sales. The survey is there to explain the likely cause, the contributing factors and the sensible next step, not to funnel every issue into the same product or repair.

faqs.question: Do you work for homeowners and buyers?
faqs.answer: Yes. Homeowners and buyers are a core audience for this kind of work because they often need clarity before spending money or progressing with a purchase.

faqs.question: Do you also support landlords and property professionals?
faqs.answer: Yes. The same technical approach can be applied for landlords, housing providers, managing agents, developers and commercial clients where evidence and reporting discipline matter.

faqs.question: How much does an independent damp survey cost?
faqs.answer: The brief suggests typical public market expectations in the region of a few hundred pounds for a comprehensive survey, but the exact cost depends on the property, complexity, and whether specialist investigation or reporting is required.

faqs.question: Why pay for a survey when some companies offer free inspections?
faqs.answer: Because free inspections are often tied to a treatment sale. Paying for an independent opinion can prevent the wrong work, avoid unnecessary expense and produce a clearer basis for the decision that follows.

faqs.question: Can you help with pre-purchase or pre-sale concerns?
faqs.answer: Yes. Independent reporting can be especially useful where damp has been flagged in a purchase, where a lender or surveyor has raised concerns, or where clarity is needed before works or a sale.

faqs.question: Can you tell the difference between rising damp, penetrating damp and condensation?
faqs.answer: Yes. Distinguishing between those mechanisms is one of the main reasons to commission a proper investigation. The correct answer depends on the building, the readings, the defect pattern and the broader context.

faqs.question: Do you deal with black mould and condensation problems?
faqs.answer: Yes. That includes ventilation, humidity, dew-point behaviour, occupancy patterns, building defects and the wider reasons mould is recurring.

faqs.question: Can you look at basements and below-ground waterproofing?
faqs.answer: Yes. Below-ground cases often need a more specialist waterproofing lens, especially where BS 8102 thinking or previous waterproofing systems are part of the picture.

faqs.question: What does the report actually help me do?
faqs.answer: A good report should help you make a better decision. That may mean deciding whether work is needed, what kind of specialist should do it, what should be prioritised, or whether previous advice was wrong.

faqs.question: Can you review someone else's report or failed treatment?
faqs.answer: Yes. Second-opinion work is especially valuable where the original explanation did not resolve the problem or the proposed solution does not seem to match the evidence.

faqs.question: Do you provide practical next-step guidance if works are needed?
faqs.answer: Yes. The goal is not to stop at diagnosis, but to explain what the sensible remedial direction looks like and what should be considered before work begins.

hero.title: Damp Problems We Diagnose (Not Just Where We Work)
hero.subtitle:
We provide independent, evidence-led damp and moisture investigations across residential, heritage and complex buildings.
Our focus is not location — it’s getting the diagnosis right.

intro.title: Most damp issues are not what they first appear to be.
intro.body:
What is often diagnosed as rising damp is frequently condensation, salt contamination, or external moisture ingress. Misdiagnosis leads to unnecessary work, wasted cost, and unresolved problems.

Our role is to identify the actual moisture mechanism affecting the building — not to apply a generic solution.

We work across London, the Home Counties and further afield on instruction, supporting homeowners, buyers, landlords, insurers and commercial clients.

problems.title: Condensation Misdiagnosed as Rising Damp
problems.body:
Surface moisture forming on cold walls is one of the most common causes of damp — and one of the most misdiagnosed.

Condensation occurs when warm, moisture-laden air meets cold surfaces. This is driven by ventilation, insulation and internal humidity — not moisture rising through the structure.

Treatments focused on injection or replastering do not resolve the issue. The correct approach involves managing airflow, temperature and moisture balance within the building.

problems.title: Hygroscopic Salts Misread as Active Damp
problems.body:
Salt-contaminated materials can absorb moisture directly from the air, creating persistent damp readings even in dry conditions.

This is commonly found in chimney breasts, older buildings, or areas affected by historic damp or contamination.

Moisture meters will often show high readings, leading to incorrect diagnosis as rising damp. In reality, there may be no active water ingress.

The solution is not chemical injection — it is proper identification and management of salt-contaminated materials.

problems.title: Penetrating Damp from External Defects
problems.body:
Water entering through defects in the building fabric is frequently diagnosed internally, without addressing the source.

Common causes include:

Failed or blocked gutters and downpipes
Cracked render or defective pointing
Poor detailing around windows and doors
Bridged cavities or construction defects

Without identifying the external pathway, internal treatments will not resolve the issue.

problems.title: Thermal Bridging Mistaken for Damp
problems.body:
Cold surfaces caused by insulation gaps, structural junctions or poor thermal performance often create condensation and mould.

Thermal imaging highlights temperature differences — not moisture — and must be interpreted correctly.

Cold spots do not automatically indicate damp. They often indicate where condensation is forming due to thermal imbalance within the structure.

problems.title: “Rising Damp” That Isn’t Rising Damp
problems.body:
True rising damp is often overstated and incorrectly diagnosed.

In many cases, low-level moisture is caused by:

Raised external ground levels
Bridged damp proof courses
External moisture loading against the wall base
Poor drainage or surface water management

Without a full external assessment, these are frequently mistaken for rising damp.

problems.title: Mould That Keeps Coming Back
problems.body:
Mould is not the problem — it is the result of moisture imbalance within the building.

Cleaning and redecoration may remove visible growth, but unless the underlying conditions are addressed, mould will return.

Key drivers include:

High internal humidity
Cold surfaces
Limited airflow and ventilation

Long-term resolution requires correcting these environmental factors.

problems.title: Damp Patches That Move or Change Shape
problems.body:
Damp that appears to move, spread or change shape without rainfall is often linked to condensation behaviour rather than leaks.

As internal conditions fluctuate, moisture forms on the coldest surfaces — shifting location depending on temperature and humidity.

This is commonly mistaken for active water ingress when it is actually a function of internal moisture dynamics.

problems.title: Drainage and External Moisture Problems
problems.body:
External water management is one of the most overlooked causes of internal damp.

This includes:

Blocked or broken drainage systems
Poor falls or water pooling against the property
Decking, patios or landscaping bridging the DPC
Surface water directing moisture towards the building

These issues can create internal symptoms that are often misdiagnosed without external inspection.

problems.title: Failed or Incorrect Previous Treatments
problems.body:
We are regularly instructed to assess properties where previous damp treatments have not resolved the issue.

This typically includes:

Chemical damp proof courses applied incorrectly
Tanking systems trapping moisture
Internal works carried out without addressing external causes

In most cases, the problem is not the product — it is the original diagnosis.

approach.title: Our Approach
approach.body:
We do not sell treatments.

We provide independent, evidence-led diagnosis based on:

Building pathology principles
British Standards (including BS 5250 and BS 6576)
Moisture behaviour and environmental assessment
Thermal imaging and moisture profiling
External and internal correlation

Our role is to identify cause, explain behaviour, and provide clear, practical next steps.

storyPanels.title: Understand the environment, not just the stain
storyPanels.body:
Where mould or air quality is part of the issue, we assess the full environment — not just the visible symptoms.

Ventilation, humidity behaviour, thermal bridging, and building use all play a role.

We identify what is actually driving the issue so it can be resolved properly — not temporarily masked.

cards.description:
We don’t begin with a treatment in mind.

Every investigation starts with how the building is constructed, how it’s being used, and how moisture is actually behaving within it.

That’s how we avoid misdiagnosis — and why our conclusions stand up.

cards.title: We follow the evidence, not assumptions

This is why we are often instructed after conflicting or incorrect advice.

faqTeaser.title: Common questions
faqTeaser.subtitle: Straight answers before you commit to anything

items.question: Why pay for an independent survey?
items.answer:
Because the goal is accurate diagnosis — not selling a treatment.

Most damp issues are misdiagnosed. We make sure yours isn’t.

items.question: Do you carry out remedial works?
items.answer:
Our role is to identify the correct solution first.

Where works are required, we help define and guide them — ensuring they align with the actual cause.

closing: Whether inspecting a single home or advising a national housing provider, our focus remains the same — clarity, accuracy, and independence.
Every investigation follows a structured, evidence-led approach — from initial readings through to clear conclusions and next steps.
We combine data, building knowledge, and practical experience to ensure what is identified — and what is recommended — is right first time.

cta.title: Still not sure what’s going on?
cta.description:
Most clients contact us when something doesn’t quite add up — or when previous advice hasn’t resolved the issue.

Speak to us directly and we’ll point you in the right direction.

items.title: Clear Diagnosis, Not Assumptions
items.desc: Clients consistently highlight the clarity of explanation — understanding what is happening within the building and why.

items.title: Root Cause Identified
items.desc: Issues are assessed in terms of moisture behaviour and building performance, not just surface symptoms.

items.title: Detailed Reporting
items.desc: Reports are structured to support decision-making, contractor pricing, and where required, insurance submissions.

latestReviews.title: Real Projects. Real Outcomes.
latestReviews.description:
These are structured summaries of recent client feedback, grouped around the types of issues we investigate most frequently.

Each reflects a different moisture mechanism or diagnostic scenario.

whatTheseReviewsShow.title: What These Reviews Actually Show
whatTheseReviewsShow.description:
Across recent projects, clients typically come to us after:

Conflicting advice from contractors
Treatments that haven’t worked
Ongoing damp or mould despite previous work
Uncertainty around what is actually causing the issue

The feedback reflects not just service quality, but correct identification of the underlying problem.

whereTypicallyInstructed.title: Where We Are Typically Instructed
items: Damp has been incorrectly diagnosed as rising damp
Moisture readings are high but the cause is unclear
Buyers require an independent assessment before purchase
Insurers require structured reporting for escape of water claims
Heritage or complex buildings require a more considered approach

seoSection.title: Damp and Moisture Investigations Across Residential and Complex Buildings
seoSection.description:
We undertake damp and moisture assessments across a wide range of properties, including:

Residential homes and flats
Period and heritage buildings
Converted properties and basements
Mixed-use and commercial spaces
Properties affected by leaks, condensation, or structural defects

Our approach is based on building pathology and moisture behaviour, not product-led solutions.

finalCta.title: If You’re Not Getting Clear Answers, That’s the Problem
finalCta.description:
Most damp issues are not resolved by treatment alone — they are resolved by understanding the cause.

If you’re unsure what’s actually happening within your property, we can provide a clear, independent assessment.

serviceAreas.title: Where we work
serviceAreas.description:
We operate across London and the Home Counties, including Hertfordshire, Buckinghamshire, Bedfordshire, Midlands and Oxfordshire.

We also take on specialist projects further afield where a clear, diagnosis-led approach is required.

cta.title: Need a clear answer, not a guess or a generic quote?
cta.description: We provide proper diagnosis so you can understand what’s happening and what should be done — rather than jumping straight into unnecessary works.

projectGallery.title: Real Investigations. Real Outcomes.
projectGallery.description: Examples of how we identify the cause and guide the correct solution.

projects.title: Grade II listed terrace — external moisture, not rising damp
projects.description: A mid-19th century listed property where previous contractors had proposed chemical damp proofing and internal tanking. Our investigation found the moisture was caused by external water management failures and lateral ingress, not rising damp. We recommended a source-first strategy focusing on external drainage rather than internal barriers that could damage the historic fabric.

projects.title: Escape of water in converted factory flat
projects.description: Visible floor distortion, fungal growth at the bathroom doorway, and ceiling staining in the property below. Our investigation identified a concealed leak behind the WC installation within a service void. Water had migrated laterally through the floor structure and vertically downwards. We provided a clear remedial plan covering leak repair, structural drying, and reinstatement.

projects.title: Listed barn conversion — no active dampness confirmed
projects.description: A Grade II listed agricultural barn conversion flagged for suspected dampness and timber deterioration in a prior RICS Level 3 survey. Our investigation found no evidence of active moisture defects — the readings were consistent with the inherent characteristics of traditional vapour-permeable construction. We recommended ongoing monitoring and conservation-aligned maintenance rather than unnecessary treatment.

projects.title: Second opinion overturns recommended damp treatment
projects.description: A homeowner had been quoted for chemical damp proofing and membrane installation by a treatment company. Our independent survey identified external defect and rainwater ingress as the primary moisture source — not rising damp. By addressing the external drainage and junction detailing, the moisture issue was resolved without any internal treatment or disruption.

projects.title: Repeated mould complaint traced to ventilation failure
projects.description: A landlord facing repeated mould complaints from tenants. Previous contractors had treated the mould cosmetically without identifying the cause. Our assessment found the mould was driven by inadequate ventilation and excess humidity, not building defects. We provided a clear ventilation strategy and monitoring plan that resolved the issue at a fraction of the previous quoted cost.

projects.title: Remedial specification for complex basement moisture
projects.description: A property with complex below-ground moisture issues where multiple contractors had given conflicting advice. Our BS 8102-aligned investigation identified the specific moisture pathways and provided a clear, prioritised remedial specification. The client was able to instruct the right specialist works with confidence and less guesswork.
